what_it_does: This file implements the missing caller/orchestrator for the existing activation, rollup, mode-detection and sense drivers. It provides a stateful ActivationCaller that runs background_tick, RollupDriver.tick, propose_mode and (only when supplied) sense_tick on each tick, aggregates each driver's legible outcome into a TickResult, and exposes an autonomous background loop that is gated off by default via the COMPANY_ACTIVATION_LOOP environment variable.
observations: The autonomous background loop is OFF by default and must be armed via COMPANY_ACTIVATION_LOOP env var.
The manual activation_tick function and any associated POST endpoint remain always available.
ActivationCaller holds one long-lived RollupDriver so its since cursor persists across ticks.
sense_tick is only invoked when a real sense_event dict is supplied; the autonomous loop passes none.
TickResult.as_dict recursively coerces nested dataclasses to dicts for JSON serialization.
Env knobs are read at call time, not import time, so the operator can flip them without restart.
The loop catches per-iteration exceptions only to keep the daemon alive and emits a warning event; it does not swallow real errors inside activation_tick.
standouts: The dormancy-by-default design is treated as a first-class safety posture, mirroring wire arming.
Persistent rollup cursor held across manual and autonomous ticks prevents re-consolidation of every wave.
Clear clock-driven vs event-only separation for sense_tick.
conventions: Heavy docstrings explain intent and safety invariants.
Dataclass-based result containers with explicit as_dict serialization.
Call-time environment configuration with safe defaults.
Reuse-only orchestration: no reimplementation of driver logic, no direct raw entry points.
concerns: run_activation_loop uses a broad except Exception (annotated noqa BLE001) and silently ignores failures in the warning emit fallback.
_coerce is defined as a nested function inside as_dict rather than a reusable utility.
maybe_start_activation_loop imports threading locally, which is fine but less discoverable.
ActivationCaller.idle_seconds is gated on None, so an override of 0 cannot be passed.
notes: A safe-by-default orchestration layer that keeps always-on cognition dormant unless the operator explicitly arms it via env var.
questions: What exact return shapes do _act.background_tick, _act.sense_tick, _act.propose_mode and _act.RollupDriver.tick produce?
What is the full interface of suite and how does bridge.py construct the shared ActivationCaller?
How does the mode detector's toggle_result action differ between off, suggest and auto modes?
Which five non-consequential destination kinds are enforced by the reused drivers?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the missing always-on caller that orchestrates existing H/I drivers while remaining dormant by default; the file matches that purpose.
intention_signals: The file is trying to close the activation loop gap without introducing new decision logic or unsafe autonomy, by wrapping existing drivers in a testable, env-gated caller that only self-runs when explicitly armed.
novelty: The explicit 'dormant + opt-in' posture is treated as a core design constraint rather than an afterthought, with the held rollup cursor and aggregated TickResult making the caller observable and serializable.
contribution: A reusable, stateful orchestration caller that drives existing activation/rollup/mode/sense drivers, persists rollup cursor state across ticks, returns a JSON-safe aggregated result, and provides an opt-in autonomous loop seam that bridge.py can spawn when armed.
summary_for_embedding: This file defines ActivationCaller, a stateful orchestrator that runs background, rollup, mode-detection and event-only sense drivers per tick, returns a JSON-safe TickResult, and exposes an env-gated autonomous loop that is dormant by default.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the missing always-on caller seam for existing cognition drivers while keeping autonomous background ticking opt-in and safe by construction.

symbols.does: Returns whether the autonomous activation loop is armed via COMPANY_ACTIVATION_LOOP env var.
symbols.description: A call-time gate that reads COMPANY_ACTIVATION_LOOP and returns true only for tokens like 1, true, yes, on, or enabled. It is the wire_armed analog that keeps the always-on loop dormant by default.

symbols.does: Returns the autonomous loop cadence in seconds from COMPANY_ACTIVATION_TICK_S.
symbols.description: Reads the env var at call time, floors the value at 1.0 second to prevent busy-spinning, and falls back to DEFAULT_TICK_SECONDS (60.0) on invalid input.

symbols.does: Dataclass that captures every driver's outcome for one activation tick.
symbols.description: Holds background, rollup, mode and sense sub-results, an aggregated reasons list, an acted flag, and wall-clock duration. It provides a structured artifact of a tick without adding new semantics.

symbols.does: Returns a JSON-safe dict projection of the tick result.
symbols.description: Recursively coerces nested dataclasses to plain dicts so the bridge endpoint can serialize the result with json.dumps without inventing a new shape.

symbols.does: Nested helper that recursively converts dataclasses, dicts, lists and tuples to JSON-safe values.
symbols.description: Used only inside as_dict to handle ActivationResult dataclass instances returned by the reused drivers.

symbols.does: Stateful orchestrator that holds the long-lived RollupDriver and runs one activation tick.
symbols.description: Constructed by bridge.py as a module-level instance shared between the manual endpoint and the autonomous loop. It reuses the existing H/I drivers byte-for-byte and never reimplements them.

symbols.does: Initializes the persistent RollupDriver with since=-1.
symbols.description: Creates the single held-cursor rollup driver so that subsequent ticks consolidate only new cognition waves rather than the entire log.

symbols.does: Runs one orchestrated tick through background, rollup, mode and optional sense drivers.
symbols.description: Always invokes background_tick, RollupDriver.tick and propose_mode; invokes sense_tick only if a real sense_event is supplied. Aggregates legible non-fire reasons and sets acted when something actually fires or switches.

symbols.does: Performs one autonomous-loop clock tick with no sense event.
symbols.description: Factored out so tests can drive a loop iteration directly without spawning a thread or sleeping. Optionally calls an on_tick observer with the result.

symbols.does: Daemon body that repeatedly ticks the caller while the env gate is armed.
symbols.description: Sleeps between ticks at the configured cadence, re-checks activation_loop_enabled each iteration, and catches per-iteration exceptions only to keep the daemon alive while emitting a loud warning event.

symbols.does: Spawns the autonomous daemon thread only if the env gate is enabled.
symbols.description: Returns None and starts nothing by default; when armed it starts a daemon=True thread named activation-caller-loop. This is the guarded spawn seam intended to be called once by bridge.py.
